text.title: go-channels Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-channels as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building a pipeline where one stage forgets to include the context.Done() case in its select statement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: That stage will block indefinitely on send if the consumer disappears, causing a goroutine leak.

failure-mode.description: Closing the fan-in output channel inside the worker loop instead of waiting for all workers to complete.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some workers may still be sending when the output is closed, causing a panic.

failure-mode.description: The classic tee snippet shadows the names out1 and out2 without renaming, then uses the same names inside the inner select.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This is a known footgun in the Go community; agents copy it verbatim and it works by accident because the shadowed variables are the ones you want, but it is fragile and confusing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Panics. Always ensure the sender never closes; only the receiver-side goroutine that owns the channel may close it.

failure-mode.description: Attempting to close a receive-only channel. This is a compile error, but only if you use directional types.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The compiler prevents this if you use receive-only types in your function signature. Bidirectional channels allow accidental close.

failure-mode.description: Using range on an unbuffered channel whose producer never closes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Range waits for close(); if the sender never closes, range blocks forever.

failure-mode.description: Buffered channels masking backpressure. Setting a buffer size without a documented capacity rationale.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Once the buffer fills, send blocks silently. The upstream stage stalls and the root cause is hard to diagnose. Always document why you buffered.

failure-mode.description: Using select with a default case for real work to poll without blocking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: This creates a busy-wait loop that burns CPU. Use a timer or block normally instead.

failure-mode.description: Goroutine leaks in fan-in: workers block on send forever if the consumer disappeared.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always include context.Done() in send operations inside workers so they can exit when cancelled.
